calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-05-2005 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-05-2005 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter came regularly on calendar this day, by assignment from the Department of the Presiding Judge for Court Trial/Prove-Up.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Leticia Perez.
Plaintiff/Cross Defendant: David Grassi dba Professional Concrete Work, present in pro per, was sworn and direct examined.
No appearance by the Defendant/Cross Plaintiff: Gregory Lowe.
Exhibit 1 - Proposal & Contract dtd. 11-13-04-------------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 2 - Proposal & Contract dtd. 12-27-04-------------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 3 - Copy of Cancelled Checks----------------------------------ID/EVID.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant, and judgment is in favor of the Cross Defendant as to the Cross Complaint.
Plaintiff to prepare and submit a blank Proposed Judgment for Complaint and Cross Complaint.
Plaintiff stipulated for the return of the Exhibits in open court.

Dec. 23, 2005
Judgment granted signed by Comm. Kaplan, see scanned doc. for details of Judgment.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-30-2005 Off Calendar

Amended Motion To Compel Gregory Lowe To Attend His Deposition
Off Calendar Nov-22-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David Grassi plaintiff and moving party in propria persona
TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE: Gregory Lowe defendant and opposing party in propria persona

After hearing, deposition ordered for November 29, 2005 at 2 p.m. in Department 610, subject to continuance of trial. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Motion To Compel Gregory Lowe To Attend His Deposition
Off Calendar Oct-18-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David Grassi plaintiff in propria persona

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
Plaintiff's motion to compel attendance at deposition is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005 requires that motions be served and filed at least 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed on September 22, 2005. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-07-2005 Set For Trial On Dec-05-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206
